articles.title: Sony Pictures Makes $100M Strategic Investment in Immersive Venue Company Cosm
articles.summary: Sony Pictures Entertainment is acquiring a minority stake in Cosm, a technology company building end-to-end immersive location-based entertainment experiences, with Sony's CEO joining Cosm's board. The $100 million infusion will accelerate Cosm's expansion of LBE venues and enhance its sports and entertainment technology platform. This signals major studio capital continuing to flow into the LBE sector, validating the premium shared-experience model that FEC operators are increasingly competing with and learning from.

articles.title: DIVR Labs Debuts New VR Game 'Castle Crusade' for Bowling Centers at Bowl Expo 2026
articles.summary: DIVR Labs will showcase Castle Crusade, a new multiplayer game for its self-service InstaVR platform, at Bowl Expo 2026 in Nashville from June 28 to July 2. The InstaVR system requires no dedicated staff to operate, making it a low-labor, high-ROI add-on attraction for bowling centers and other FEC venues. Operators attending Bowl Expo can demo the product at Booth #875 alongside InstaVR Arena and InstaVR GO.

articles.title: Semnox's Parafait Platform Powers New Multi-Attraction FEC in Maryland
articles.summary: K&K Adventure Zone in Prince Frederick, Maryland opened with an arcade, laser tag, VR, mini golf, and laser maze running on Semnox's Parafait platform, deployed alongside Betson Enterprises. The setup includes LuminOS game readers, self-service kiosks, and a centralized POS system enabling cashless gameplay across all attractions. The deployment offers a real-world case study for FEC operators evaluating integrated technology stacks for multi-attraction venues.

articles.title: Hasbro-Branded Family Entertainment Center Coming to Pigeon Forge in Summer 2027
articles.summary: A Hasbro-branded family entertainment center is set to open in Pigeon Forge, Tennessee in summer 2027, bringing a major toy and game IP into the FEC space. The announcement signals growing interest from consumer brands in licensing their intellectual property for immersive, location-based family entertainment. FEC operators and developers should watch this trend as branded venues increasingly compete for family leisure spending.

articles.title: Monster Mini Golf Launches Custom App with Score Tracking, Party Reservations, and Gamification
articles.summary: Monster Mini Golf has rolled out a new branded mobile app and connected digital experience platform across its locations nationwide, adding features like score tracking, birthday party reservations, and gamified guest engagement. The platform is designed to drive repeat visitation and personalize the guest experience at the glow-in-the-dark entertainment brand's venues. This is a useful benchmark for FEC operators exploring how branded apps can deepen guest loyalty and streamline operations.

articles.title: Wavegarden Analysis Shows Surf Lagoons Delivering Up to 9x Equity Multiples and 10x Land Value Gains
articles.summary: A new analysis by Wavegarden reports that its surf lagoon projects are generating project IRRs as high as 178% and land-value increases of up to 10x across the U.S. and five continents. The findings position surf lagoons as high-yield anchor attractions that boost surrounding real estate and drive significant investor returns. FEC developers and investors evaluating large-format water attractions should take note of these financial performance metrics.

articles.title: Holovis Plans 40+ New Hires Amid Surge in Demand for Complex Immersive Attraction Technology
articles.summary: Experience design and systems integration firm Holovis is expanding its global team by over 40 positions across the UK, USA, Middle East, and Asia, plus new remote roles in Manchester, Bristol, and Austin. The hiring surge reflects rising demand from attractions and entertainment venues for sophisticated immersive technology solutions. For FEC operators considering large-scale immersive upgrades, Holovis's growth signals a healthy supplier ecosystem with expanding capacity.

articles.title: PICO and STYLY Partner to Scale VR Content Distribution Across Location-Based Entertainment
articles.summary: STYLY has selected the PICO 4 Ultra Enterprise headset as its hardware platform of choice for a long-term LBE distribution partnership, citing ease of use for non-technical staff and visitors ranging from young children to seniors. The partnership expands STYLY's reach as a global LBE content distribution platform, giving FEC operators more turnkey VR experience options. The focus on staff simplicity and broad visitor accessibility makes this particularly relevant for FEC operators running lean teams.

articles.title: Kokomo Joe's FEC Adding Multi-Level Go-Kart Track
articles.summary: Kokomo Joe's, a family entertainment center, is investing in a multi-level go-kart installation, one of the most sought-after premium attractions in the FEC sector. Multi-level tracks are known for increasing dwell time, driving repeat visits, and differentiating venues in competitive markets. This is a concrete example of FEC operators reinvesting in signature anchor attractions to stay competitive.

articles.title: Bingemans Expands Big Splash Waterpark to 13 Slides with New Typhoon Twister Tower
articles.summary: Bingemans in Kitchener, Ontario has launched Typhoon Twister, a new four-slide tower featuring twists, drops, racing elements, and spinning experiences, bringing Big Splash to 13 total waterslides. The expansion reinforces the venue's position as Waterloo Region's largest outdoor waterpark heading into summer 2026. For FEC and waterpark operators, the addition illustrates how tiered attraction additions can meaningfully expand a venue's appeal and capacity.

articles.title: Star Factory Lantern Promotes Day-to-Night Dinosaur Strategy to Extend Guest Dwell Time
articles.summary: Star Factory Lantern is marketing a combined daytime animatronic dinosaur and nighttime illuminated lantern experience designed to help parks extend operating hours, increase secondary spending, and deepen family engagement. The dual-format attraction strategy is positioned as a revenue multiplier for parks and FEC operators looking to activate their outdoor spaces after dark. This approach is increasingly relevant for FECs seeking to maximize revenue per guest visit.

articles.title: Belt Entertainment & Volleys FEC Announces Closure
articles.summary: Belt Entertainment & Volleys, a family entertainment and sports venue, has announced it will close its doors, marking another FEC casualty in the current market environment. Closures of multi-attraction venues highlight ongoing challenges around operational costs, competition, and post-pandemic demand shifts facing independent FEC operators. Industry stakeholders should monitor closure patterns as indicators of broader market health.

articles.title: Tornado Terry's Family Amusement Center in Fort Worth to Close After 30 Years
articles.summary: Tornado Terry's Family Amusement Center in Fort Worth, Texas is shutting down after three decades in operation, according to local reports. The closure of a long-standing, family-owned FEC underscores the pressure facing independent operators competing against newer, larger entertainment concepts. This story will resonate with FEC owners weighing reinvestment versus exit decisions in today's competitive landscape.

articles.title: Europa-Park's Immersive Dark Ride Dining Experience Eatrenalin Earns First Michelin Star
articles.summary: Eatrenalin, the world-first multi-sensory restaurant inside Germany's Europa-Park where guests dine on dark ride vehicles through an 8-course meal, has been awarded its first Michelin star by the Michelin Guide Germany. The recognition elevates the concept of experiential F&B as a serious revenue and brand-building driver within attraction venues. FEC operators investing in elevated dining experiences can point to Eatrenalin as proof that food and entertainment integration can achieve world-class distinction.

articles.title: ProFun Academy Announces Aquatic Safety Training Partnership with Ellis & Associates
articles.summary: The ProFun Academy in Riyadh has partnered with Safety Skills Training DMCC, the international training arm of Ellis & Associates, to deliver aquatic safety and lifeguard training for attractions operators. The partnership reflects growing emphasis on professional safety standards in the Middle East's rapidly expanding entertainment and attractions industry. FEC operators running waterpark or aquatic attractions should be aware of this expanding training resource.

articles.title: New Indoor Slide and Air Court Park Opening in Overland Park, Kansas This Summer
articles.summary: A new indoor entertainment venue featuring slides and air courts is set to open in Overland Park this summer, adding to the growing market of active indoor play destinations targeting families. The opening reflects continued investor appetite for new-format indoor entertainment concepts in suburban markets. FEC operators in the region will face new competition, while suppliers can identify a potential new client.

articles.title: New Adventure Park Described as Unlike Anything in the Country Opens in Atlanta
articles.summary: A new adventure park has opened in Atlanta with operators claiming it offers experiences unlike any other venue in the country, suggesting a differentiated attraction mix. Atlanta's growing entertainment market makes it a bellwether for new FEC concepts gaining traction in major metro areas. FEC operators and investors tracking emerging venue formats should follow this opening closely.

articles.title: Big Air Indoor Trampoline Park Opens New Location in Glendale, Arizona
articles.summary: Big Air, an indoor trampoline park brand, is opening a new location in Glendale, Arizona, continuing the chain's expansion across U.S. markets. Trampoline park operators and suppliers can use this opening to gauge franchise growth momentum and competitive density in the Southwest. The Glendale opening also adds data points for understanding site selection trends in the active entertainment segment.

articles.title: Chalk & Steel Co. Plans New Indoor Entertainment Venue in Keller, Texas
articles.summary: Chalk & Steel Co. is bringing a new indoor entertainment venue to Keller, a growing suburb in the Dallas-Fort Worth metro area, according to local reports. The DFW market continues to attract new FEC investment, reflecting strong family demographics and consumer spending in the region. Operators and suppliers eyeing the Texas market should note this new entrant.

articles.title: High Tide VR Brings Location-Based Virtual Reality Entertainment to Delaware Beach Market
articles.summary: High Tide VR, a location-based virtual reality venue near Bethany Beach, Delaware, is profiled as offering a differentiated entertainment alternative in a traditional beach tourism market. The venue illustrates how VR attractions can carve out viable niches in seasonal, tourist-heavy environments where traditional FEC formats may not fit. FEC operators and VR suppliers exploring non-traditional markets will find this a useful case study.

articles.title: New Entertainment Facility Set to Open in Springfield, Illinois in July
articles.summary: A new entertainment facility is scheduled to open in Springfield, Illinois in July, adding to the mid-sized market's leisure options. While details are limited, new venue openings in mid-market cities are useful indicators of where FEC investment is flowing outside of major metros. Suppliers and operators tracking market expansion should keep an eye on this opening.

articles.title: Splish Splash Water Park Hosts Early-Access Morning for Autism Society Members
articles.summary: New York's Splish Splash water park is hosting a dedicated early-morning opening for the Nassau/Suffolk Chapter of the Autism Society of America, giving members two hours of low-stress access before regular park hours begin. The initiative is a strong example of inclusive programming that FEC operators can adapt to build community goodwill and serve guests with sensory sensitivities. Sensory-friendly events are an increasingly recognized best practice across the FEC and attractions industry.

articles.title: Lake Compounce Launches All-American Summer Celebration for America's 250th Anniversary
articles.summary: Lake Compounce in Bristol, Connecticut is marking its historic 180th season and America's 250th birthday with a summer-long event featuring fireworks, drone shows, patriotic entertainment, specialty food, and military appreciation programming. The multi-layered event strategy demonstrates how parks can layer seasonal programming to drive repeat visits and PR coverage during a milestone cultural moment. FEC operators can draw inspiration from this approach to themed seasonal activations.

articles.title: Masti Zone Opens New Family Entertainment Destination in Greater Noida West, India
articles.summary: Masti Zone has opened a new family entertainment destination at PKS Town Central in Greater Noida West, expanding the brand's footprint in one of India's fastest-growing suburban markets. The opening reflects the strong global growth trajectory for FEC concepts in emerging markets with large, young family demographics. International FEC operators and suppliers should note India as an accelerating opportunity market.

articles.title: Arizona Media Ranks Top 10 Indoor Entertainment Attractions for 2026
articles.summary: AZ Big Media has published its annual ranking of the top 10 indoor entertainment attractions in Arizona for 2026, offering a snapshot of which venue types and brands are resonating with consumers in one of the U.S.'s fastest-growing states. Rankings like this influence consumer choice and can impact foot traffic for both listed and unlisted competitors. FEC operators in the Southwest and suppliers targeting the Arizona market will find the list a useful competitive intelligence tool.
